name: geniml
tagline: Guides genomic-interval ML workflows (Region2Vec, BEDspace, scEmbed, universe building) via the geniml Python package.
verdict: Clean, plain-text documentation skill for the legitimate open-source geniml package. No hidden instructions, credential access, destructive commands, or obfuscation. Minor notes: it offers an optional git+https dev install and a separate third-party StarSpace install (normal for scientific tooling but slightly less verifiable than PyPI), and it doesn't spell

highlights.summary: Line 31 offers `uv pip install git+https://github.com/databio/geniml.git` (dev version bypassing PyPI review) and lines 310-312 direct users to separately install StarSpace from facebookresearch/StarS
No explicit negative-trigger guidance (e.g., 'not for non-genomic data' or 'not for supervised classification tasks') anywhere in the file.
Individual code snippets show output artifacts (e.g. embeddings.npy, universe.bed) but the skill never states an explicit overall output contract for what the agent should return to the user.
